name: Gold and Silver Traders
description_short: Seattle Gold buys and sells precious metals including gold, silver, diamonds, and bullion at competitive prices. Family-owned operation with locations in Seattle and Portland plus mail-in services.
description_long:
Seattle Gold is a family-owned precious metals buyer and seller operating in Seattle, Washington and Portland, Oregon. The company was established to provide an alternative to traditional pawn shops and predatory cash-for-gold operations, positioning itself as a transparent, ethics-focused precious metals broker. They maintain physical retail locations and offer a mail-in service for customers unable to visit in person.

The company buys and sells a wide range of precious metals and items including gold jewelry, silver jewelry and flatware, gold and silver bullion (coins, bars, ingots), dental gold, platinum, palladium, diamonds, and old bank notes. They display real-time commodity prices on their website (gold, silver, platinum, palladium) and emphasize a three-step selling process: bringing items in for a free quote, having items assessed with transparent explanation of valuation methodology, and receiving a written offer before sale completion. They also offer a mail-in service explicitly marketed as a safer alternative to other cash-for-gold operations.

Seattle Gold differentiates itself through several claimed advantages: a "best price guarantee" promising to match or beat competitor offers, emphasis on family ownership and ethical sourcing (particularly for diamonds over 0.25 carat, which are GIA certified), a transparent pricing system based on purity and weight plus current market conditions, and positioning as "the largest network of neighborhood gold buyers and sellers." The company notes in an April update that they are well-capitalized and continue purchasing both gold and silver even when other businesses have paused such purchases.

As a precious metals buyer, Seattle Gold operates in a commodity-based market where prices fluctuate daily. Their business model depends on customers having unwanted precious metals to sell. The guarantee to beat competitor prices is valuable but depends on customers shopping around. The mail-in service addresses geographic limitations but introduces shipping and insurance considerations. The company's actual market position and pricing competitiveness would require independent verification against other local precious metals buyers.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Best price guarantee—will match or beat competitor offers if customers bring higher quotes
Real-time precious metals pricing displayed on website (gold, silver, platinum, palladium)
Transparent three-step valuation process with written offers before sale
GIA-certified diamonds for most stones over 0.25 carat
Mail-in service available as alternative to in-person visits, marketed as safer than typical cash-for-gold operations
Multiple locations (Seattle and Portland) plus national mail-in coverage
Family-owned business emphasizing ethics, transparency, and customer service
Stated commitment to continue purchasing during market disruptions (silver purchasing noted during pause by competitors)
cons: Pricing is commodity-based and fluctuates daily—best offer today may not match tomorrow's market
Best price guarantee is only valid if customers independently verify competitor offers; no published pricing comparison
Mail-in service requires shipping of valuable items with associated insurance and transit time considerations
Limited to customers who have precious metals to sell; not suitable for those seeking loans or credit products
Physical locations limited to Seattle/Portland area despite mail-in option
best_for: People with unwanted gold, silver, or diamond jewelry they want to convert to cash
Those seeking bullion sales or liquidation of precious metal collections
Customers in Seattle or Portland seeking local precious metals buyers with transparent pricing
Remote sellers comfortable with mail-in precious metals appraisal and payment services
diagnosis: Seattle Gold is best for individuals holding unwanted precious metals (jewelry, bullion, coins, diamonds) who want to sell them locally with transparent pricing and best-price guarantees, or remotely via mail-in service. The main caveat is that this is a commodity-based business—prices fluctuate daily with market conditions, and the actual competitiveness of their pricing depends on comparing multiple local buyers, not just their guarantee promise.
services: Purchase of gold jewelry and bullion (coins, bars, ingots)
Purchase of silver jewelry, flatware, and bullion
Diamond buying with GIA certification for stones over 0.25 carat
Free written quotes on precious metals
Sale of bullion and precious metals to customers
Best price guarantee matching competitor offers
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
